title: pneumonanthe parvifolia
intro: Pneumonanthe parvifolia, also known as lungwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. Lungwort has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, offering health benefits like relieving congestion and promoting a healthy cough. The herbal preparations of lungwort include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and provide relief from respiratory symptoms.
